description: The Marine Management Organisation (MMO) was created in 2009 by the Marine and Coastal Access Act. MMO is an executive non-departmental public body, sponsored by the Department for Environment, Food & Rural Affairs .

details.body:
The Marine Management Organisation (MMO) was created in 2009 by the Marine and Coastal Access Act.

MMO is an executive non-departmental public body, sponsored by the Department for Environment, Food & Rural Affairs.

ordered_featured_documents.summary: Fishers with an interest in the crawfish fishery in English waters of ICES area 7 are being urged to respond to a new online survey on proposals for a seasonal closure in 2026/2027.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Crawfish consultation 2026

ordered_featured_documents.summary: The owner and a master of a UK-registered fishing vessel have both been sentenced following a joint enforcement operation and subsequent inspections carried out at the Port of Blyth in 2024 and 2025.
ordered_featured_documents.title: MMO and NIFCA joint enforcement operation results in successful prosecution

ordered_featured_documents.summary: Fishermen, seafood businesses, and coastal communities can now apply for funding through the relaunched Fisheries and Seafood Scheme (FaSS), funded from the over £132 million committed to the Fishing and Coastal Growth Fund over the next five years.
ordered_featured_documents.title: £132 million UK Government investment open to the fishing and seafood industries and coastal communities across England

ordered_featured_documents.summary: Queen scallop fishing in ICES sub areas 6a and 7a will be closed from 1 April to 30 June 2026 to protect spawning stocks.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Temporary queen scallops closure

ordered_featured_documents.summary: Skippers and masters who succeed in this year’s application process for a bluefin tuna (BFT) catch and release permit for English waters will now have to undertake mandatory training
ordered_featured_documents.title: Bluefin tuna fisheries 2026

ordered_featured_documents.summary: Marine Management Organisation (MMO) has today 23 March 2026 announced its decision on a temporary closure to dredge fishing for king scallops in ICES areas 7d and 7e in 2026.
ordered_featured_documents.title: King scallop dredge fishery temporary closure decision

details.body: The Director of Digital and Analysis works with a range of stakeholders and provides multi-disciplinary technical services for the MMO, which focus on understanding the data and information that we gather and using it to inform our decisions and shape the future direction of our marine service provision for our customers. The Director is leading delivery to realise the MMO’s ambitions in evidence use and to ensure our services are fit for purpose and using ‘digital by default’ to further support the customer offering.

details.body:
The Director of Marine Development (Domestic and International) is accountable for enabling sustainable development in England’s marine area, and supporting global marine protection.

This includes developing and implementing plans for England’s marine area, developing evidence and approaches to optimise its use for government policy ambition including renewable energy, and ensuring integration in service-focussed marine licensing decision making.

Additionally, domestic expertise and experience as England’s primary marine regulator is used to provide advice and assistance internationally, through Government programmes such as Blue Belt and Ocean Country Partnership Programme. The Director has accountability for our role as a delivery partner across these programmes.

role.title: Director of Marine Development (Domestic and International)

role_appointments.title: Trudi Wakelin - Director of Marine Licensing, Planning and Blue Belt, MMO

role_appointments.title: Non-executive Board Member, MMO Mark Ripley - Non-executive Board Member, MMO

details.body:
Government Finance Function

Mark is a director in the Government Finance Function and leads the GFF’s Risk & Assurance Centre of Excellence work to support key aspects of their priorities covering:

  • Getting the basics right: Sound forecasting and reporting, with robust data, efficient transaction processing, and effective management of risk, supported by standards, policies, guidance and strong functional leadership
  • People, diversity and capability: A high performing and diverse function, with great people in the right roles with the right skills
  • Planning, risk and performance: Driving a strong culture of planning, risk and performance with integrated financial and business planning, aligned with robust risk and assurance

role_appointments.title: Mark Ripley - Risk & Assurance Director

details.body:
Responsibilities include:

  • growing volunteering, social action, community action and philanthropy

  • creating the conditions for a more independent and resilient voluntary, community and social enterprise sector

  • promoting social investment and social impact bonds, helping to develop a capital market that supports social impact

  • leading across government on the development of mutuals

  • developing government policy on mission-led businesses

  • supporting youth policy, leading on the creation of opportunities outside school for young people to develop skills and have a stake in society

  • expanding the National Citizen Service

  • libraries policy

  • First World War commemorations

role.title: Parliamentary Under Secretary of State for Civil Society

role_appointments.title: Rob Wilson - Parliamentary Under Secretary of State for Civil Society

details.body:
The Minister for Civil Society leads on the civil society agenda. The minister is responsible for:

  • the Big Society agenda
  • National Citizen Service and youth policy
  • social action
  • civil society sector support
  • social enterprise and social investment

The minister is also responsible for UK statistics, and supports the Minister for the Cabinet Office on public sector efficiency and reform.

role_appointments.title: Rob Wilson - Parliamentary Secretary (Minister for Civil Society)

details.body: Chief Strategy Officer is responsible for ensuring focusing on specific, time‑limited priorities and supporting the ongoing transition and handover arrangements currently underway.
The Chief Executive Officer, MMO is responsible for growth of the organisation by deciding and directing the strategic priorities in regulation and planning.
The Director of Operations, MMO has overall responsibility for coastal operations, marine compliance, and statistics and fisheries management.

Evidence, data and technical advice are required for Marine Management Organisation (MMO) operational functions. These can consist of:

  • quality assuring evidence presented by licence applicants
  • peer reviewing licensing advice provided by advisors or applicants
  • acquiring or procuring new data or information assets to underpin MMO decisions
  • providing technical advice on a range of topics to support MMO functions
  • commissioning research or technical advice for marine planning, licensing, conservation enforcement and fisheries management

The Marine Management Organisation is committed to developing and maintain effective working relations with our partner organisations, to ensure a joined-up approach to cost-effective, efficient, transparent and well informed management and regulation of our seas. The Director, Customers and Partnerships is accountable for this and for our exceptional customer service.

The Chair of the Marine Management Organisation (MMO) leads the Board in its fulfilment of its collective responsibilities and discharging of its statutory duties to ensure that the MMO’s policies and actions support Government’s wider strategic policies.

Non-executive Board Members of the Marine Management Organisation (MMO) have collective responsibility for the strategic direction and overall performance of the organisation, ensuring that it is properly and effectively managed and providing stewardship for the public funds entrusted to it.

details.body: The Chief Operating Officer is responsible for the operations directorate overseeing all coastal operations, fisheries management, compliance and enforcement functions.

The Deputy Chair of the Marine Management Organisation (MMO) helps to lead the Board in its fulfilment of its collective responsibilities and discharging of its statutory duties to ensure that the MMO’s policies and actions support Government’s wider strategic policies.

The Director of Operations, MMO has overall responsibility for coastal and offshore operations, enabling and assuring marine compliance, and helping to develop future fisheries and marine conservation management.

The Director of Digital and Analysis works with a range of stakeholders and provides multi-disciplinary technical services for the MMO, which focus on understanding the data and information that we gather and using it to inform our decisions and shape the future direction of our marine service provision for our customers. The Director is leading delivery to realise the MMO’s ambitions in evidence use and to ensure our services are fit for purpose and using ‘digital by default’ to further support the customer offering.

The Director of Marine Development (Domestic and International) is accountable for enabling sustainable development in England’s marine area, and supporting global marine protection.

This includes developing and implementing plans for England’s marine area, developing evidence and approaches to optimise its use for government policy ambition including renewable energy, and ensuring integration in service-focussed marine licensing decision making.

Additionally, domestic expertise and experience as England’s primary marine regulator is used to provide advice and assistance internationally, through Government programmes such as Blue Belt and Ocean Country Partnership Programme. The Director has accountability for our role as a delivery partner across these programmes.
